domain: Balinese gamelan kotekan interlocking parts (polos = on-beat anchor; sangsih = off-beat fill; each sparse alone; together fast composite figuration; composite tempo > 2× either solo)
specific_mechanism: Two complementary sparse sub-streams interleave temporally; neither part complete alone; composite is dense.
llm_application: KOTEKAN-INTERLEAVE: split decoding across two SUB-DECODERS that share an attention trunk but emit tokens at DISJOINT POSITIONAL SLOTS — sub-decoder A produces tokens at even positions {0,2,4,...}, sub-decoder B at odd positions {1,3,5,...}. Each sub-decoder runs at half the token rate (skipping alternate positions). Composite output forms a complete sequence. Each sub-decoder can be smaller/cheaper since it operates on a sparser distribution. Different from PASTA-style chunk parallelism (chunks are contiguous, not interleaved) and from speculative decoding (single token stream).
novelty_claim: PASTA decodes parallel CONTIGUOUS chunks. Speculative decoding has draft + verify on single stream. Multi-token prediction emits k future tokens at once but in sequence. PDT synchronizes via shared latent across parallel streams. KOTEKAN-INTERLEAVE differs by STRICT EVEN/ODD POSITIONAL ALTERNATION across two sub-decoders sharing trunk — sparse per-channel decoding with dense composite via positional slot allocation.
